name: database-lookup
tagline: Looks up facts from 78 public scientific/financial/regulatory database APIs with provenance, pagination, and rate-limit handling.
verdict: Unusually security-conscious: explicitly treats API responses as untrusted data, blocks prompt injection from returned payloads, and handles credentials narrowly (checks one named env/.env var, never dumps or echoes secrets). Minor issues only: file runs slightly over the ~500-line budget, no explicit negative-trigger guidance, and allowed-tools declares onl

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use when...' statement; only an implicit contrast ('rather than inferred from general knowledge', line 3) hints at the negative trigger.
Frontmatter allowed-tools is 'Read Bash' (line 4), but the Making API Calls section names WebFetch as the primary Claude Code fetch tool (line 313) — that tool isn't declared.
File is 534 lines, modestly over the ~500-line budget, driven by the large database-selection and identifier tables.
